maturityScale.label: Published Consumable
maturityScale.meaning: Package set can be consumed from npm and can serve both standalone and HIA integration scenarios.

maturityScale.label: Bridge Consumable
maturityScale.meaning: A published bridge or legacy-compatible package exists, while the unified doc-line umbrella remains in workspace incubation.

maturityScale.label: Local Consumable
maturityScale.meaning: Local packages and fixtures can be consumed by HIA gates, but the public release path is not complete.

maturityScale.label: Workspace Incubation
maturityScale.meaning: The doc line has package boundaries and baseline code, but packages remain private or versioned as 0.0.0.

docLines.title: JSDoc
docLines.standaloneMode: Use the published JSDoc plugin/theme pair today; the hia-jsdoc umbrella packages remain workspace-private.
docLines.hiaIntegrationMode: JSDoc output is adapted through parser-jsdoc and HIA producer contracts.
docLines.nextPublicMilestone: Publish or otherwise publicize the @hia-doc/hia-jsdoc umbrella package set after preset and plugin registry hardening.

docLines.title: TSDoc
docLines.standaloneMode: Use @hia-doc/tsdoc-runner to extract TypeScript documentation without embedding sourcesContent by default.
docLines.hiaIntegrationMode: Use @hia-doc/tsdoc-producer through the HIA project manifest producer pipeline.
docLines.nextPublicMilestone: Turn target-project recipes into public quickstarts and keep registry smoke in release gates.

docLines.title: DotNetDoc
docLines.domain: .NET XML documentation and C# source linkage
docLines.standaloneMode: Use @hia-doc/dotnetdoc-runner over .NET XML documentation output and source relation inputs.
docLines.hiaIntegrationMode: Use @hia-doc/dotnetdoc-producer through the HIA project manifest producer pipeline.
docLines.nextPublicMilestone: Add Roslyn/direct-source extraction path while retaining XML documentation as the stable baseline.

docLines.title: HTMDoc
docLines.standaloneMode: Local runner/extractor package boundaries exist but remain private workspace packages.
docLines.hiaIntegrationMode: HTMDoc producer participates in HIA self-doc gates through explicit producer configuration.
docLines.nextPublicMilestone: Promote core runner/spec packages from private workspace to local-consumable release candidates.

docLines.title: CSSDoc
docLines.domain: CSS selectors, components, properties and design tokens
docLines.standaloneMode: Local runner/extractor package boundaries exist but remain private workspace packages.
docLines.hiaIntegrationMode: CSSDoc producer participates in HIA self-doc gates through explicit producer configuration.
docLines.nextPublicMilestone: Stabilize CSSDoc spec surface and decide first public package release train.

docLines.title: PugDoc
docLines.domain: Pug templates and generated HTML documentation linkage
docLines.standaloneMode: Local Pug extractor/runner package boundaries exist but remain private workspace packages.
docLines.hiaIntegrationMode: PugDoc bridges generated HTML semantics into HTMDoc/HIA producer artifacts.
docLines.nextPublicMilestone: Move include/extends/block coverage from fixture evidence into release candidate gates.

docLines.title: SassDoc
docLines.domain: Sass sources and generated CSS documentation linkage
docLines.standaloneMode: Local Sass extractor/runner package boundaries exist but remain private workspace packages.
docLines.hiaIntegrationMode: SassDoc bridges generated CSS semantics into CSSDoc/HIA producer artifacts.
docLines.nextPublicMilestone: Stabilize Sass/CSSDoc bridge fixtures before public release candidate packaging.

docLines.title: VueDoc
docLines.standaloneMode: Local Vue SFC runner and producer packages exist as non-private 0.0.0 candidates.
docLines.hiaIntegrationMode: VueDoc producer models SFC template/script/style relations as a framework bridge over HTMDoc/CSSDoc/TSDoc concepts.
adoptionEvidence: HIA self-doc Vue SFC producer fixture
docLines.nextPublicMilestone: Choose whether VueDoc P1 is released before or after broader framework bridge hardening.

recipes.summary: Install the TSDoc runner, extract selected TypeScript entries, and keep generated artifacts out of source-control decisions until the target project opts in.
Start from compiler XML documentation, add optional source/ASP.NET surface inputs, and feed artifacts into HIA unified output when needed.

cases.title: UnicodeArtJs TypeScript Documentation Adoption

publicNarrative.problem: A TypeScript project needs standalone documentation extraction and a path into unified HIA output without committing generated private source content.
publicNarrative.solution: Use @hia-doc/tsdoc-runner for target-project extraction, then feed the produced artifacts into HIA project aggregation when the project wants unified HTML output.
publicNarrative.currentBoundary: The evidence is read-only and maintainer-notification-driven; the target repository decides whether to pull and absorb candidate config or CI.

cases.nextAction: Convert the read-only candidate into a public quickstart and, if the target project opts in, write a maintainer-side notify message instead of editing target files directly.

cases.title: HIA-ASPNETPortal .NET Documentation Adoption

publicNarrative.problem: A .NET Framework/Web Forms project needs API documentation, XML documentation intake, source relation, and endpoint surface evidence without requiring immediate source modifications.
publicNarrative.solution: Use @hia-doc/dotnetdoc-runner over compiler XML documentation and optional source/ASP.NET surface inputs, then feed artifacts into HIA unified output.
publicNarrative.currentBoundary: XML documentation is the stable baseline; Roslyn/source and ASP.NET endpoint bridges continue to deepen in later cycles.

cases.nextAction: Prepare a public .NET quickstart that starts with XML documentation and clearly marks Roslyn/source and ASP.NET endpoint bridges as progressive enhancements.

releaseHistory.notes: Current deployed public reference candidate. Historical multi-version navigation is not yet enabled.

hostAnchors.summary: HIA host surfaces reuse the same project index, relation graph, source-linkage query, and structured open request contracts so IDE, DevTools, browser-panel, and AI-assisted authoring can share one documentation context.
principles: The portal describes host capabilities without claiming marketplace-ready or fully confirmed GUI behavior.
Host surfaces consume generated public artifacts and sanitized relation metadata instead of parsing private workspace notes.
AI-assisted authoring should use explicit source-linked context, locale contracts, diagnostics, and reviewable edits.

concepts.title: Project Relation Graph
concepts.summary: A generated graph that connects documentation entries, source ranges, generated artifacts, semantic members, and framework endpoints.

concepts.title: Source Linkage
concepts.summary: A query boundary for moving between documentation symbols, original files, generated artifacts, ordinary source maps, and doc-source-map manifests.

concepts.title: Structured Open Request
concepts.summary: A host-neutral request shape for opening source, generated artifacts, documentation entries, or previews in the user's chosen tool.

surfaces.title: Language Server Protocol
surfaces.currentBoundary: Profile-defined rule execution and richer project status views remain planned.
surfaces.nextPublicMilestone: Use the relation graph request as the common service layer for IDE and editor adapters.

surfaces.title: VS Code
surfaces.currentBoundary: Visible Extension Development Host confirmation is still required before calling this complete.
surfaces.nextPublicMilestone: Turn prepared runtime evidence into visible navigation confirmation and user-facing status surfaces.

surfaces.title: Browser Panel
surfaces.currentBoundary: The panel is a local reference surface, not a browser store extension.
surfaces.nextPublicMilestone: Keep it as a stable payload contract for DevTools and portal previews.

surfaces.title: Browser DevTools
surfaces.currentBoundary: No content script overlay or native source opener is claimed in the current public state.
surfaces.nextPublicMilestone: Promote the shell into a productized DevTools bridge after W-P29 host hardening.

surfaces.title: Multi-IDE Boundary
surfaces.currentBoundary: Visual Studio, JetBrains, and Neovim adapters are planned boundaries rather than shipped plugins.
surfaces.nextPublicMilestone: Start Visual Studio as a dedicated .NET-oriented host project and keep LSP as the shared base.

aiAssistedAuthoring.title: AI-Assisted Documentation Authoring
aiAssistedAuthoring.summary: The long-term workflow is to let AI find documentation gaps, propose bilingual comments, update translations, and explain source-linked context while human authors keep review control in IDE and DevTools surfaces.
workflowSteps: Index project entries, relation graph nodes, diagnostics, and locale metadata.
Select bounded source-linked context for the requested documentation task.
Generate or revise documentation comments and translations as reviewable candidates.
Run profile, privacy, bilingual, route, and source-linkage gates before accepting changes.
guardrails: No hidden private workspace input in public artifacts.
No direct target-repository modification without opt-in.
No language-core vendor prefix when a standard-level name is appropriate.
aiAssistedAuthoring.nextPublicMilestone: Connect the workflow to W-P29 IDE/DevTools host productization and later generic language abstraction.

artifactContracts.title: Project Index
artifactContracts.role: Unified project navigation, search metadata, locale metadata, and relation graph carrier.
artifactContracts.currentBoundary: The index is generated output; hosts should consume it as data, not scrape renderer HTML.

artifactContracts.title: Project Relation Graph
artifactContracts.role: Connects entries, source ranges, generated artifacts, semantic members, and framework endpoints.
artifactContracts.currentBoundary: The graph is a navigation model, not a guarantee that every host can open every target today.

artifactContracts.title: Documentation Source Map
artifactContracts.role: Maps documentation entries back to original source and generated artifacts without embedding source text by default.
artifactContracts.currentBoundary: sourcesContent remains none unless a project explicitly opts into a stricter private workflow.

artifactContracts.title: Structured Open Request
artifactContracts.role: Host-neutral message for opening source, generated artifacts, documentation entries, or previews.
artifactContracts.currentBoundary: The request describes intent; each host decides how much native navigation it can safely provide.

artifactContracts.title: Browser Panel Payload
artifactContracts.role: Static browser-facing relation graph, source-linkage, and open-request preview payload.
artifactContracts.currentBoundary: This remains a local/reference payload and is not a public browser store extension package.

evidenceMatrix.publicEvidence: W-P22.5 target host evidence validated UnicodeArtJs and HIA-ASPNETPortal project-index consumption through LSP boundaries.
evidenceMatrix.notClaimed: No direct editor UI, marketplace package, or profile rule execution is claimed by the LSP boundary alone.

evidenceMatrix.publicEvidence: W-P23.2 prepared Extension Development Host evidence covers relation choices and open target reports for target project artifacts.
evidenceMatrix.notClaimed: Visible Extension Development Host confirmation and packaged extension release are still future productization work.

evidenceMatrix.publicEvidence: W-P22.4 and W-P22.5 validated browser-panel payload generation and target project relation graph consumption.
evidenceMatrix.notClaimed: It does not claim native file opening, content-script overlay, or browser store distribution.

evidenceMatrix.publicEvidence: W-P23.3 added a local unpacked DevTools shell that renders browser-panel payload relation data.
evidenceMatrix.notClaimed: No content-script overlay, source panel integration, or Chrome Web Store release is claimed.

evidenceMatrix.publicEvidence: W-P23.5 established the multi-IDE compatibility boundary and the Visual Studio hybrid route.
evidenceMatrix.notClaimed: Visual Studio, JetBrains, and Neovim adapters are planned host projects, not shipped plugins.

wP29Inputs: Confirm visible VS Code Extension Development Host navigation with real target artifacts.
Productize the DevTools panel shell while preserving browser-panel payload compatibility.
Turn LSP relation graph requests into the shared host service layer for editor adapters.
Start Visual Studio host planning as a .NET-oriented dedicated project without blocking LSP reuse.
Connect AI-assisted authoring to source-linked diagnostics and reviewable edit proposals.

feedback.summary: Feedback surfaces collect public-safe portal, compatibility, adoption, package and host findings without treating them as completed external ecosystem adoption.
publicBoundaries: Feedback must use public URLs, package names, versions, public-safe environment summaries and sanitized logs.
Private source code, private repository paths, credentials and embedded sourcesContent are out of scope for public issues.
A D4 candidate needs a repeatable public-safe consumer record before it can be counted as external adoption evidence.

issueTemplates.title: Public Portal Feedback
issueTemplates.notFor: Private project debugging or requests that require modifying a target repository.

issueTemplates.title: Compatibility Or Adoption Report
issueTemplates.notFor: Publishing credentials, private source excerpts, or unsanitized build artifacts.

compatibilityNotes.title: Maturity Labels Are Evidence Scoped
compatibilityNotes.summary: A published-consumable doc line means its package set can be consumed from npm for a reviewed scenario; it does not imply complete language coverage.
compatibilityNotes.currentBoundary: Maturity labels describe the current public evidence floor, not a permanent compatibility guarantee.

compatibilityNotes.title: Runner Versions Must Stay Explicit
compatibilityNotes.summary: Adoption recipes should name the minimum runner or producer version used by the evidence, so target projects can reproduce the same contract.
compatibilityNotes.currentBoundary: Floating latest installs are acceptable for experiments but not sufficient for D4 evidence.

compatibilityNotes.title: Source Content Privacy Remains Default
compatibilityNotes.summary: Documentation source maps and public portal evidence should omit sourcesContent unless a project explicitly opts into a stricter private workflow.
compatibilityNotes.currentBoundary: Public feedback should use sanitized snippets, counts, URLs and package versions instead of private source payloads.

compatibilityNotes.title: Host Capability Claims Stay Surface Specific
compatibilityNotes.summary: A relation graph or structured open request proves data availability for a host; it does not prove every GUI can open every target until that host has runtime evidence.
compatibilityNotes.currentBoundary: Host compatibility findings must identify the exact surface and supported flow involved.

d4CandidateBacklog.title: Public Third-Party Consumer
evidenceNeeded: a public project or branch outside the HIA repository set
d4CandidateBacklog.currentBoundary: Current target-project evidence is useful but still curated by the HIA workspace process.

d4CandidateBacklog.title: Published Package Repeatability
evidenceNeeded: isolated install or packed consumer smoke
d4CandidateBacklog.currentBoundary: TSDoc and DotNetDoc have published-package smoke; broader doc lines still need the same public repeatability floor.

d4CandidateBacklog.title: Public Feedback Loop
evidenceNeeded: issue forms available in the public repository
triage labels or equivalent maintainer workflow
d4CandidateBacklog.currentBoundary: Issue templates and portal pages prepare the loop; they do not prove external feedback volume.

d4CandidateBacklog.title: Host Consumer Feedback
evidenceNeeded: relation graph or open-request payload involved
d4CandidateBacklog.currentBoundary: W-P28.5 exposes host evidence anchors; W-P29 must productize host runtime before stronger claims.
